title: Mouthwatering Flaky Moroccan Beef Shin Sellou
description: A traditional Moroccan dish featuring slow-cooked beef shin in a rich and flavorful sellou crust, reduced to perfection and served with a side of vegetables, making it a vegetarian-friendly option by substituting the beef with portobello mushrooms for a vegetarian version.

ingredients.name: beef shin
ingredients.notes: or 2 large portobello mushrooms for vegetarian version

instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the beef shin with salt, black pepper, cumin, and paprika.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Sear the beef shin until browned on all sides, then set aside.
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger to the pot and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the sellou crust mix to the pot and stir to combine.
Return the beef shin to the pot and cover with a lid.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and cook for 2 1/2 hours.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Serve the beef shin with the sellou crust and a side of roasted vegetables.
